name.es: Pan de masa madre de San Francisco
name.fr: Pain au levain de San Francisco

description.en: The bread that gave sourdough its name in the popular imagination. A rye-seeded culture rich in Lactobacillus sanfranciscensis, cool Bay Area-style fermentation and a days-long cold schedule build a sharp, unmistakable tang beneath a thick, crackling crust.
description.de: Das Brot, dem der Sauerteig seinen Ruf verdankt. Eine mit Roggen angesetzte Kultur reich an Lactobacillus sanfranciscensis, kühle Gärung nach Art der Bay Area und ein tagelanger Kälteplan erzeugen eine scharfe, unverwechselbare Säure unter einer dicken, krachenden Kruste.
description.es: El pan que dio a la masa madre su fama popular. Un cultivo con centeno, rico en Lactobacillus sanfranciscensis, una fermentación fresca al estilo de la bahía y un proceso frío de días crean un sabor ácido intenso e inconfundible bajo una corteza gruesa y crujiente.
description.fr: Le pain qui a rendu le levain célèbre dans l'imaginaire populaire. Une culture au seigle riche en Lactobacillus sanfranciscensis, une fermentation fraîche façon baie de San Francisco et un long repos au froid produisent une acidité franche sous une croûte épaisse et craquante.

origin.note: The tang comes from a specific wild-yeast/bacteria partnership favored by the Bay Area's cool, foggy climate, first studied and named after the city in the 1970s.

keeping.note: The sourness deepens over the first day or two.

method.title: Feed the culture
method.summary: Combine a rye-seeded sourdough culture with bread flour and water; ferment 9 hours at room temperature, then refrigerate 34 hours to build a sharply sour levain.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Stir the cold levain into water, then work in the bread and spelt flours and salt; knead briefly and rest 15 minutes.

method.title: Short bulk with folds
method.summary: Perform two stretch-and-fold sequences 15 minutes apart, then let the dough rest about 40 minutes at room temperature.

method.title: Cold retard the dough
method.summary: Refrigerate the shaped-but-unformed dough for about 15 hours to deepen the sourness.

method.title: Acclimate and shape
method.summary: Let the dough warm up 2 hours at room temperature, then shape into a batard or boule.

method.title: Final proof
method.summary: Proof 2.5-3 hours at room temperature until it passes the poke test.

method.title: Bake with steam
method.summary: Score and bake at 235°C on a hot stone or steel with steam (a splash of hot water in a tray) for about 45 minutes, venting the door near the end for a crisp crust.

method.title: Cool
method.summary: Rest fully before slicing to let the tangy crumb set.

source.adaptation: Baker's percentages recomputed to include the levain-building flour and water in the 100% flour base per house convention.
